notes: Hikari is the Japanese word ŚÖČ ("light; ray") and the name behind HikariCP, the high-performance JDBC connection pool that Spring Boot uses by default ‚ÄĒ hence the ubiquitous "HikariPool-1 - Starting..." log line. The README's own heading spells the pronunciation out as Hi¬∑ka¬∑ri [hi¬∑ka¬∑'lńď]: hee-kah-REE, three even syllables with the Japanese flapped r sounding close to an English l. English readers commonly get it wrong as "HIGH-kah-ree" with a long English i; the trailing CP (Connection Pool) is then spelled out as C-P.
